Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class RestClient

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.RestClient
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, AutoCloseable
public final class RestClient extends Object implements Closeable
Next-generation transport-agnostic REST client.

Create instances via create() or builder():

// Minimal — uses the built-in JDK HttpClient transport RestClient client = RestClient.create(); // Explicit transport (e.g. for tests) RestClient client = RestClient.builder() .transport(mockTransport) .header("Accept", "application/json") .build();

By default, RestClient uses JavaHttpTransport (backed by the JDK's HttpClient), which ships built into the juneau-rest-client artifact and requires no extra dependencies on Java 11+. Pulling in one of the optional transport modules (juneau-rest-client-apache-httpclient-45, -apache-httpclient-50, -okhttp, -jetty) registers a higher-priority provider via ServiceLoader that takes over automatically. An explicit RestClient.Builder.transport(HttpTransport) call always wins.

See Also:
  • juneau-ng REST client
Since:
9.2.1

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_BODY_CONVERTERS

      public static final List<BodyConverter<?>> DEFAULT_BODY_CONVERTERS
      Default body converters applied in order when RestRequest.body(Object) is called.

      Custom converters registered via RestClient.Builder.bodyConverter(BodyConverter[]) are prepended to this list. Call RestClient.Builder.bodyConverters(BodyConverter[]) to replace all defaults.

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static RestClient create()
      Creates a new RestClient using auto-discovered transport.
      Returns:
      A new instance. Never null.

    • builder

      public static RestClient.Builder builder()

    • get

      public RestRequest get(String url)
      Creates a GET request to the given URL.
      Parameters:
      url - The target URL. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new RestRequest. Never null.

    • request

      public RestRequest request(String method, String url)
      Creates a request with the given HTTP method and URL.
      Parameters:
      method - The HTTP method. Must not be null.
      url - The target URL. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A new RestRequest. Never null.

    • getTransport

      public HttpTransport getTransport()
      Returns the underlying transport used by this client.
      Returns:
      The transport. Never null.

    • getDefaultSerializer

      public Optional<Serializer> getDefaultSerializer()
      Returns the explicitly-configured default serializer used for outbound bodies when the format is not otherwise discernable.

      Only an explicit default set via RestClient.Builder.defaultSerializer(Serializer) is honored — there is no implicit JSON fallback and no lone-registered-entry fallback. A fully-unconfigured client returns Optional.empty().

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

      Returns:
      The configured default serializer, or Optional.empty() if none was set.

    • getMatchingParser

      public Optional<Parser> getMatchingParser(String contentType)
      Returns the parser matching the given response Content-Type, or the explicitly-configured default parser.

      Resolution precedence: an exact media-type match against the client's ParserSet wins; otherwise the explicitly-configured default parser is used; otherwise Optional.empty() is returned. There is no implicit JSON fallback and no lone-registered-entry fallback, so a fully-unconfigured client resolves to empty (callers throw 415 Unsupported Media Type).

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

      Parameters:
      contentType - The response Content-Type header value. May be null.
      Returns:
      The matching parser, or Optional.empty() if nothing matched and no default parser is configured.

    • getSerializerForMediaType

      public Optional<Serializer> getSerializerForMediaType(String mediaType)
      Returns the registered request serializer matching the given media type, or Optional.empty() if none matches.

      Unlike getDefaultSerializer(), this method performs media-type-driven selection against the client's SerializerSet (via SerializerSet.getSerializer(String)) and returns Optional.empty() on no match so the caller can apply the locked no-match fallback (use the default serializer but still send the overridden Content-Type label). An explicitly-configured single default serializer is also consulted when no set is registered.

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*).

      Parameters:
      mediaType - The desired request media type. May be null/empty.
      Returns:
      The matching serializer, or Optional.empty() if no registered serializer matches.

    • getParserForMediaType

      public Optional<Parser> getParserForMediaType(String mediaType)
      Returns the registered parser matching the given media type, or Optional.empty() if none matches.

      Used by the next-gen RemoteClient as the accept fallback parser: it is consulted only when the response Content-Type matched no registered parser (or the response was unlabeled). Returns Optional.empty() on no match so the caller can fall back to the default parser.

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*).

      Parameters:
      mediaType - The desired parse media type. May be null/empty.
      Returns:
      The matching parser, or Optional.empty() if no registered parser matches.

    • getDefaultAccept

      public String getDefaultAccept()
      Returns the default Accept header value advertising what this client can read.

      Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

      Returns:
      The default Accept header value, or null if no parsers and no default parser are configured (no implicit application/json fallback).

    • remote

      public <T> T remote(Class<T> iface)
      Creates a Java proxy for the given Remote-annotated interface.

      Each method call on the returned proxy will be translated into an HTTP request using this client.

      UserService svc = client.remote(UserService.class); String user = svc.getUser("42");

      Type Parameters:
      T - The interface type.
      Parameters:
      iface - The interface class. Must be annotated with Remote. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A proxy instance backed by this client. Never null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If iface is not an interface or not annotated with @Remote.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Throws:
      IOException
